---
output: pdf_document
---
# DeepSeek V3 0324 vs OpenAI: GPT-4o-mini
```{r prompt-histograms-individual, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary package
library(ggplot2)
library(pwr)

# Beispiel: 15 Gruppen, mittlerer Effekt, 0.05 Signifikanzniveau, 0.8 Power
pwr.chisq.test(w = 0.3, df = 14, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.8)

# Read the CSV file
data1 <- read.csv("school_responses.csv")
data2 <- read.csv("school_responses_openai_gpt-4o-mini_500requests_20250513_144843.csv")
data3 <- read.csv("school_responses_x-ai_grok-3-mini-beta_500.csv")

categories <- c(
 "Gymnasium",
        "Realgymnasium",
        "Wirtschaftskundliches Realgymnasium",
        "Höhere technische und gewerbliche Lehranstalt",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Mode",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Kunst und Gestaltung",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Produktmanagement und Präsentation",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Tourismus",
        "Handelsakademie",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für wirtschaftliche Berufe",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Pflege und Sozialbetreuung",
        "Höhere Lehranstalt für Land- und Forstwirtschaft",
        "Bildungsanstalt für Elementarpädagogik",
        "Bildungsanstalt für Sozialpädagogik"
)


#Count the amount of industries
observed1 <- table(factor(data1$school, levels = categories))
observed2 <- table(factor(data2$school, levels = categories))
observed3 <- table(factor(data3$school, levels = categories))

#Expected distribution of the data
expected_probs <- rep(1 / length(categories), length(categories))

#Chi Squared Test
chisq.test(x = observed1, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed2, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed3, p = expected_probs)

# Cramers V for observed1
X1 <- 1304.7
df1 <- 13
k1 <- df1 + 1
N1 <- sum(observed1)
V1 <- sqrt(X1 / (N1 * (k1 - 1)))

print(V1)

# For Cramers V observed2
X2 <- 729.22
df2 <- 13
k2 <- df2 + 1
N2 <- sum(observed2)
V2 <- sqrt(X2 / (N2 * (k2 - 1)))
print(V2)

# For Cramers V observed3
X3 <- 3483.6
df3 <- 13
k3 <- df3 + 1
N3 <- sum(observed3)
V3 <- sqrt(X3 / (N3 * (k3 - 1)))
print(V3)

# Create a bar chart of school frequencies
ggplot(data1, aes(x = school)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per School (DeepSeek)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of school frequencies
ggplot(data2, aes(x = school)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per School (ChatGPT)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of school frequencies
ggplot(data3, aes(x = school)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per School (Grok)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
</div></pre>
